headline: BofA's Erica AI Assistant Surpasses 2 Billion Interactions, Serving 42 Million Clients Since Launch
summary: Bank of America's AI-powered virtual assistant Erica has surpassed 2 billion total interactions since its launch, serving 42 million clients. The milestone underscores the scale at which BofA has deployed conversational AI across its consumer banking platform. Erica is accessible through the Bank of America mobile app and provides clients with financial guidance, account information, and transaction support.

verified_claims: Erica has surpassed 2 billion total interactions since launch
The product is named 'Erica' and is Bank of America's AI-powered virtual assistant
The milestone headline matches the official BofA press release title exactly
unverified_claims: Erica provides financial guidance, account information, and transaction support — implied by context but not explicitly confirmed in the visible portion of the source
Erica is accessible through the Bank of America mobile app — plausible and commonly known, but not confirmed in the visible source snippet
_governance.notes: The two core statistics (2 billion interactions, 42 million clients) are directly confirmed by the BofA official press release headline and URL. The summary's additional descriptive claims about Erica's capabilities and mobile app accessibility are reasonable and widely reported, but the source document was truncated before the body of the press release could be verified. No contradictions detected. Confidence slightly reduced due to incomplete source rendering.
